guides.title: Car Accident
guides.summary: Stay calm. Prioritize safety. Document everything.

steps.instruction: Stop immediately and turn on hazard lights.
Check yourself for injuries. Do not move if you feel severe pain.
Check on other passengers. Do not move injured persons unless they are in immediate danger.
Call 911 if there are injuries. Call 117 (PNP) to file a police report.
If the vehicle is drivable and no one is injured, move it to the road shoulder.
Document the scene: take photos of vehicle positions, damage, and license plates.
Exchange name, contact number, license plate, and insurance information with the other driver.
Collect contact information from any witnesses.
Notify your insurance provider as soon as possible.

doNot: Do not flee the scene.
Do not argue or confront the other driver.
Do not move severely injured persons.
Do not sign any documents at the scene.

guides.title: Medical Emergency
guides.summary: Call for help first. Stay with the person. Keep them still.

steps.instruction: Call 911 immediately. Do not wait.
Stay with the person. Do not leave them alone.
Keep the person still and comfortable. Help them sit or lie down.
Loosen tight clothing around the neck and chest.
Do not give food, water, or medication unless instructed by emergency services.
If the person is unconscious and not breathing, begin CPR if you are trained.
Keep bystanders away — give the person space and air.
When emergency responders arrive, describe the symptoms and how long they have been present.

doNot: Do not move the person unless absolutely necessary.
Do not give food, water, or medicine on your own.
Do not leave the person alone while waiting for help.
Do not panic visibly in front of the person.

guides.title: Fire
guides.summary: Get everyone out. Call for help. Do not re-enter.

steps.instruction: Alert everyone nearby. Shout 'Fire!' clearly.
Activate the nearest fire alarm if one is available.
Evacuate immediately using the nearest safe exit. Do not use elevators.
Close doors behind you as you leave — this slows the spread of fire and smoke.
Stay low if there is smoke. Crawl if necessary — smoke rises.
Call 160 (Bureau of Fire Protection) or 911 once you are safely outside.
Go to your designated assembly area and account for all people.
Do not re-enter the building for any reason.
Cooperate fully with firefighters when they arrive.

doNot: Do not use elevators during a fire.
Do not re-enter the building for any reason.
Do not waste time gathering belongings.
Do not open a door if the handle feels hot.

guides.title: Crime / Robbery
guides.summary: Your safety comes first. Do not resist. Report after.

steps.instruction: Prioritize your safety. Do not resist or fight back against armed perpetrators.
Comply calmly. Most perpetrators want property, not confrontation.
Once the incident is over, move to a safe location away from the scene.
Call 911 or 117 (PNP) as soon as you are safe.
Do not disturb the crime scene — do not touch or move anything.
Write down everything you remember: descriptions, vehicle details, direction the suspects fled.
Identify and collect contact information from any witnesses.
File a police report as soon as possible. Request a copy for insurance and legal records.

doNot: Do not resist armed perpetrators.
Do not pursue or follow the suspects.
Do not disturb the crime scene.
Do not post on social media before filing a report.

guides.title: Flood / Disaster Aftermath
guides.summary: Move to higher ground. Contact authorities. Wait for the all-clear.

steps.instruction: Move immediately to higher ground or an upper floor if flooding is active.
Do not walk or drive through floodwaters. Even shallow moving water is dangerous.
Turn off electricity at the main breaker if water is entering your home.
Call 136 (MMDA) or 911 if you need rescue assistance.
Contact your barangay hall or local DRRMO to register your location and status.
Avoid drinking tap water until authorities confirm it is safe.
Do not return home until authorities issue an official all-clear.
Document all property damage with photos before cleaning up — for insurance claims.

doNot: Do not walk or drive through floodwater.
Do not touch electrical equipment in flooded areas.
Do not return home without the official all-clear.
Do not drink tap water without confirmation it is safe.
